name: pdebug
transitions: real->protected (CR0.PE 0->1, CS far-jump to 32-bit flat code segment 0x08)
IDT load (lidt base=0x1000) + a #DB TRAP gate at vector 1 (db_handler, attr 0x8F so IF is preserved)
MOV DRn<->GPR round-trip: write DR0..DR3/DR7/DR6, read back into GPRs (observed through GPRs -> g-packet)
TF single-step #DB: POPF sets EFLAGS.TF -> the next insn (nop) takes a #DB TRAP -> db_handler (DR6.BS) -> IRET
DR0 instruction breakpoint #DB: DR0=&bp_target + DR7 L0/type00 -> #DB FAULT before bp_target -> db_handler (DR6.B0) -> disarm DR7 -> IRET restarts bp_target
DR1 data-write breakpoint #DB: DR1=wp_data + DR7 L1/type01/LEN4 -> #DB TRAP after the store -> db_handler (DR6.B1) -> disarm DR7 -> IRET resumes past the store
DR7.GD general-detect probe (STRUCTURAL): DR7.GD=1 + MOV from DR0 -> NO #DB under qemu (GD not modeled); a P5-faithful RTL WOULD #DB (DR6.BD)

oracle_feasibility.verdict: PARTIAL — MOV-DR round-trip + TF/DR0/DR1 #DB delivery are DIFFERENTIAL; DR7.GD general-detect is STRUCTURAL (qemu does not model it)
oracle_feasibility.movdr_round_trip: DIFFERENTIAL. DR values are NOT in the gdbstub g-packet (HMP-only: confirmed by 'info registers DR0..DR7'), but MOV r32,DRn deposits them into GPRs which ARE in the g-packet. Round-trip read-backs land in the golden GPRs/memory: DR0=0x11111111 DR1=0x22222222 DR2=0x33333333 DR3=0x44444444 DR7=0x00000400 (bit10 DR7_FIXED_1) DR6=0xffff0ff0 (DR6_FIXED_1 reserved-1 pattern). qemu helper_set_dr/helper_get_dr (tcg/sysemu/bpt_helper.c) implement the access; in 32-bit mode DR_RESERVED_MASK=0xffffffff00000000 has no effect (all bits writable), DR6 |= 0xffff0ff0, DR7 |= 0x400.
oracle_feasibility.tf_single_step_db: DIFFERENTIAL. #DB is a SYNCHRONOUS exception raised inside the TB (helper_single_step -> raise_exception(EXCP01_DB)), NOT an interrupt_request — so SSTEP_NOIRQ (which only masks CPU_INTERRUPT_SSTEP_MASK = SMI/IRQ in cpu-exec.c:817-819) does NOT swallow it (unlike SMM/SMI in psmm). cpu_handle_exception (cpu-exec.c:749-766) runs do_interrupt (guest IDT delivery) FIRST, THEN returns EXCP_DEBUG to gdb — so the gdbstub single-step LANDS on the guest db_handler. Empirically: nop@0xf0156 with TF=1 -> #DB TRAP -> db_handler@0xf0220, DR6.BS(0x4000) set, IRET resumes 0xf0157 (TRAP pushes NEXT eip). Deterministic across 3 runs.
oracle_feasibility.hw_breakpoint_db: DIFFERENTIAL (both DR0 instruction-bp AND DR1 data-write-bp). This DIVERGES from the spec's 'RISKY, possibly STRUCTURAL' prediction — the de-risk shows BOTH fire under single-step. Mechanism: a guest hw breakpoint is inserted as a BP_CPU cpu_breakpoint/cpu_watchpoint (hw_breakpoint_insert). Although check_for_breakpoints_slow (cpu-exec.c:342) returns false under singlestep ('singlestep overrides breakpoints'), the gdb-step EXCP_DEBUG processing runs cpu_handle_debug_exception -> breakpoint_handler (debug_excp_handler), which tests cpu_breakpoint_test(eip, BP_CPU) / the watchpoint hit and raises EXCP01_DB INTO THE GUEST (tcg/sysemu/bpt_helper.c:209-229). So the guest #DB still vectors. Empirically: DR0 inst-bp FAULT (jmp@0xf017f -> #DB before bp_target@0xf0181, DR6.B0(0x1), FAULT pushes faulting eip, IRET restarts 0xf0181); DR1 data-write TRAP (store@0xf01b1 -> #DB after, DR6.B1(0x2), TRAP pushes next eip, IRET resumes 0xf01b7). Total #DB=3, cause OR=0xffff4ff3 (BS|B0|B1). Deterministic across 4 runs. NOTE for the implement phase: x86_debug_check_breakpoint suppresses arch breakpoints when EFLAGS.RF=1 (RF disables instruction breakpoints for one insn) — the RTL must honor RF likewise.
oracle_feasibility.gd_general_detect: INFEASIBLE differentially -> IMPLEMENTED-BUT-DISABLED + DEFERRED (NOT self-checked by any gate). qemu 8.2.2 does NOT implement DR7.GD general-detect: DR7_GD (1<<13) and DR6_BD (1<<13) are DEFINED in cpu.h:274/279 but NEVER tested/set anywhere in target/i386 (grep-confirmed). helper_get_dr/helper_set_dr do no GD check. Empirically: with DR7.GD=1, MOV %dr0,%eax did NOT #DB (GD-fire witness [0x2038] stayed 0, BD count [0x203c] stayed 0, DR6 had no BD bit). A P5-faithful RTL WOULD raise #DB (DR6.BD set, FAULT before the access) — so RTL vs qemu DIVERGE here. The GD DECISION PATH is fully CODED in the RTL (core.sv ~3168: the SYS_MOVDR_TO/FROM + DR7.GD check + arm_db(DR6.BD)) but is HELD BEHIND a default-off localparam DBG_GD_ENABLE=1'b0, so with the as-built RTL the GD #DB NEVER fires: the corpus's section-(e) GD probe runs IDENTICALLY in RTL and qemu (no fault, [0x2038]/[0x203c] stay 0), the RTL takes EXACTLY 3 #DB like the golden, and the differential diff stays EQUIVALENT. The corpus EXERCISES the path (same code both oracles) but GD is EXCLUDED from the differential gate AND is NOT structurally self-checked by any wired gate (there is no TB hook to build an RTL-only DBG_GD_ENABLE=1 trace; flipping the localparam would diverge the ONLY wired gate). A real structural check (the psmm 3c/3d precedent) is DEFERRED future work.
oracle_feasibility.smm_save_map_note: Orthogonal to this stage but relevant: psmm/M2S.5 deferred DR6@+0xFFCC / DR7@+0xFFC8 in the SMM save map. M2S.6 adds the DR file; whether SMM save/restore of DR6/DR7 is wired is an M2S.5/M2S.6 seam, not exercised by pdebug.

delivery_records.tf_db_fault: n=126 pc=0x000f0156 (the nop stepped with EFLAGS.TF=1 -> #DB TRAP)
delivery_records.tf_db_enter: n=128 cs=0x08 eip=0x000f0220 (db_handler gate target; #DB delivered via the trap gate, IF preserved)
delivery_records.tf_db_dr6: db_handler reads DR6 = 0xffff4ff0 (DR6.BS bit14 set) into a GPR
delivery_records.tf_db_iret: n~ IRET resumes pc=0x000f0157 (tf_resume; TRAP pushed the NEXT eip)
delivery_records.bp0_fault: n=155 pc=0x000f017f (the jmp bp_target; #DB FAULT fires BEFORE bp_target@0x000f0181)
delivery_records.bp0_enter: n=157 cs=0x08 eip=0x000f0220 (db_handler), DR6=0xffff0ff1 (DR6.B0 bit0 set)
delivery_records.bp0_iret_restart: IRET resumes pc=0x000f0181 (bp_target; FAULT pushed the FAULTING eip; handler disarmed DR7 so the restart proceeds)
delivery_records.wp1_trap: n=192 pc=0x000f01b1 (the store to wp_data 0x3000; #DB TRAP fires AFTER the store)
delivery_records.wp1_iret_resume: IRET resumes pc=0x000f01b7 (wp_resume; TRAP pushed the NEXT eip)
delivery_records.success_proof: settled tail (n~232-236): ecx=0x3 (#DB total = BS+B0+B1) edx=0xffff4ff3 (DR6 cause OR over the reserved-1 base) eax=0x1 (BS count) ebx=0x1 (B0 count) esi=0x1 (B1 count). GD-fire witness [0x2038]=0 + BD count [0x203c]=0 under qemu (GD not modeled).
delivery_records.gd_structural: n=~187 pc reaches the MOV %dr0,%eax with DR7.GD=1 and does NOT fault under qemu (the golden shows EXACTLY 3 #DB deliveries, not 4). The GD #DB is RTL-only.

gate.differential_records: pdebug RTL --system EQUIVALENT to pdebug.sys.vtrace.golden across cr0..cr4 + the 6 selectors + GPRs + eflags + eip. This COVERS: (1) the MOV DRn<->GPR round-trip (the DR values observed THROUGH the GPRs/memory the handlers read — no DR trace field), (2) the TF single-step #DB delivery (db_handler entry CS:EIP, the pushed-frame effects, DR6.BS read into a GPR, the IRET resume), (3) the DR0 instruction-breakpoint #DB (FAULT -> faulting eip -> restart) and (4) the DR1 data-write-breakpoint #DB (TRAP -> next eip -> resume), all keyed off the existing M2S.3 IDT-delivery diff infrastructure (gate read -> frame push -> handler -> IRET) — NO trace-format change.
gate.structural_records: DR7.GD general-detect (the (e) section) is IMPLEMENTED-BUT-DISABLED and DEFERRED — it is NOT self-checked by any gate. qemu does NOT model GD, so it is EXCLUDED from the differential diff. The GD decision + #DB-fire path is CODED in the RTL but gated off behind DBG_GD_ENABLE=1'b0, so the as-built RTL takes EXACTLY 3 #DB exactly like the golden: under section (e) the RTL does NOT fault, DR6.BD stays clear, and the GD-fire witness [0x2038]/BD count [0x203c] stay 0 — IDENTICAL to qemu — which is precisely what keeps the differential diff EQUIVALENT. There is NO wired structural self-check that asserts a 4th GD #DB: doing so would require a TB hook to build an RTL-only trace with DBG_GD_ENABLE=1 (flipping the localparam diverges the only wired gate: 4 #DB + witnesses set vs the golden's 3 + witnesses clear). A psmm-style (3c/3d) RTL-only structural confirmation is DEFERRED future work. (Contrast psmm, whose structural claims ARE backed by wired self-check scripts.)
gate.selfcheck_note: If the implement phase wants a free-run structural cross-check (the psmm pattern), it can read the pdebug witnesses at 0x2000..0x203c via QMP (xp/1xw) and assert the DR round-trip + the 3 differential causes + the GD-witness-clear, as a belt-and-suspenders alongside the differential golden diff. The 4 differential parts do NOT need this (they diff against the golden directly).

description: Bare-metal real->protected DEBUG-REGISTER / #DB demonstrator for the M2S.6 PARTIAL-ORACLE stage (NO paging). Boots from F000:FFF0 in 16-bit real mode, builds a flat 3-entry GDT, sets CR0.PE and far-jumps into the 32-bit flat code segment, loads data selectors + a flat stack, zeroes a marker area at linear 0x2000, builds an IDT at linear 0x1000 with a 32-bit #DB TRAP gate (attr 0x8F, IF preserved) at vector 1 -> db_handler, and lidt's it. It then exercises the M2S.6 debug machinery: (a) MOV DRn<->GPR round-trips — write DR0..DR3/DR7/DR6 known patterns, read them back into GPRs (the DR values are HMP-only / not in the g-packet, so they are observed THROUGH GPRs, the M2S.1 hidden-base trick); (b) TF single-step #DB — POPF sets EFLAGS.TF, the next nop takes a #DB TRAP, db_handler reads DR6.BS, scrubs the saved TF, IRETs (resume at the next eip); (c) a DR0 instruction breakpoint — DR0=&bp_target + DR7 L0/type00, the jmp #DB-FAULTs before bp_target, db_handler reads DR6.B0, disarms DR7, IRET restarts the faulting eip; (d) a DR1 data-write breakpoint — DR1=wp_data(0x3000) + DR7 L1/type01/LEN4, the store #DB-TRAPs after it, db_handler reads DR6.B1, disarms DR7, IRET resumes past the store; (e) a DR7.GD general-detect probe — DR7.GD=1 + MOV from DR0, which under qemu does NOT fault (GD unmodeled). A single shared db_handler dispatches on the DR6 cause bits (BD/BS/B0/B1), accumulates per-cause counters + a cause OR-witness, clears DR6, and IRETs. The test exits via out 0xf4 (isa-debug-exit -> qemu status 133). PARTIAL ORACLE de-risk verdict (empirically established BEFORE RTL): the MOV-DR round-trip + the TF/DR0/DR1 #DB deliveries are DIFFERENTIALLY observable under the qemu-system-i386 gdbstub single-step oracle (#DB is a synchronous exception that SSTEP_NOIRQ does NOT mask, and qemu delivers it through the guest IDT before returning EXCP_DEBUG to gdb — UNLIKE the SMI of psmm; the golden captures exactly 3 #DB deliveries with the correct FAULT/TRAP eip semantics). The DR7.GD general-detect is INFEASIBLE differentially (qemu 8.2.2 does not implement GD — DR7_GD/DR6_BD are defined but never tested/set) and is IMPLEMENTED-BUT-DISABLED + DEFERRED: the GD decision/#DB path is coded in the RTL but held behind a default-off localparam DBG_GD_ENABLE=0, so the as-built RTL takes EXACTLY 3 #DB like the golden (section (e) does NOT fault, witnesses stay 0) and the differential diff stays EQUIVALENT. There is NO wired structural self-check of GD firing (no TB hook for an RTL-only DBG_GD_ENABLE=1 trace); the psmm-style structural confirmation is deferred future work. NO paging; CR0.PG stays 0. The golden pdebug.sys.vtrace.golden (239 records) is deterministic across runs and self-diffs EQUIVALENT.
